summary: Proper variable management in Next.js prevents security leaks, ensures type safety, and leverages the framework's built-in environment variable handling for both server and client contexts.
reasoning: Next.js inlines NEXT_PUBLIC_ variables at build time, so any secret prefixed this way is permanently embedded in client bundles
TypeScript strict typing catches bugs at compile time and improves developer experience with autocompletion
Environment variable validation at build time prevents runtime failures in production from missing configuration
The server/client component boundary means server-only env vars are intentionally inaccessible on the client for security
